questions.q: In mid-May 2026, what legal consequence befell a Michigan man who had previously promoted what he hoped would be the 'largest book burning' in American history?
options: He was jailed for contempt of court
He was fined by the EPA for air pollution
He was banned from all state public libraries
He was sued by a coalition of local authors
questions.correctText: He was jailed for contempt of court

questions.q: A widely circulated May 2026 article discussed the unique challenges 19th-century missionaries faced when translating the Bible into Cherokee. Which grammatical feature did the Cherokee language notably lack when translators attempted to describe God?

According to industry news from May 13, 2026, government authorities charged three individuals connected to PageTurner Press and Media for running a massive publishing scam. Approximately how much money did they defraud from authors?
As reported on May 13, 2026, Russian photographer Alexandra Kuzyk was sentenced to 18 months in a labor camp for publishing what type of unauthorized written content?
